summary: Applied `dtk dol apply` to refresh the GALE01 symbol map. The PR touched only `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`, with a large mechanical update to symbol scopes, autogenerated local labels, and some generated data labels. It appears to synchronize the repository's symbol metadata with dtk's current DOL analysis rather than change gameplay/source code.

key_files.role: Generated GALE01 symbol map refreshed by `dtk dol apply`; 1667 lines added and 1517 deleted across 172 hunks.

what_changed: `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` was regenerated or updated by running `dtk dol apply`.
Many symbols had scope metadata changed between `local` and `global` without address or size changes.

Several OS/Runtime support labels became global, including `__OSDBINTSTART`, `__OSDBIntegrator`, `__OSDBINTEND`, `__OSDBJUMPEND`, `__OSEVStart`, `OSExceptionVector`, `__OSSystemCallVector`, `__OSSystemCallVectorStart`, and `__OSSystemCallVectorEnd`.
A missing runtime label was added at the start of `OSDisableInterrupts`: `__RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin = .text:0x80347364`.

Some functions that had been marked global were corrected to local in HSD texture/shadow areas, including `TObjLoad`, `MakeTextureMtx`, `TObjSetupMtx`, `MakeColorGenTExp`, `TObjInfoInit`, `drawBackgroundRect`, and `makeMatrix`.
Some HSD texture conversion helpers were changed from local to global, including `HSD_TGTex2Index`, `HSD_TexCoord2Index`, `HSD_TexMtx2Index`, and `HSD_TexMap2Index`.
Many compiler-generated local object names changed numeric suffixes, such as `vec0_init$497` to `vec0_init$494`, `@485` to `@465`, and similar `@NNN` renumberings in `.rodata` and `.data`.
Additional generated labels like `...data.0` were introduced at some data/string addresses, for example `.data:0x803BA050`, `.data:0x803BA508`, `.data:0x803BCDC0`, and `.data:0x803C0530`.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to the generated symbol configuration file, making a large mechanical update easier to review and safer to merge.
Used the canonical tool command named in the PR title, `dtk dol apply`, instead of hand-editing hundreds of symbol metadata changes.
Preserved addresses and sizes while updating symbol scope/name metadata, which reduces risk of accidental binary layout changes.
Accepted autogenerated local-label churn where the tool changed compiler-style suffixes, keeping the checked-in symbol map aligned with the current tooling output.
decomp_lessons: When dtk symbol inference changes, commit the resulting `symbols.txt` refresh separately from source decompilation work so future diffs do not mix mechanical metadata churn with semantic code changes.

Scope metadata in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` matters: labels and functions used across translation units or exposed by runtime conventions should be global, while file-local helpers should remain local.
Do not overinterpret `@NNN` and `$NNN` compiler-generated local names; they may be renumbered by tool output and are weaker anchors than stable addresses, sizes, or manually named symbols.

Runtime/assembly boundary labels such as `__RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin`, exception-vector labels, DB-integrator labels, and system-call vector labels may need explicit symbol entries even when they alias function start/end addresses.
Generated `...data.0` labels can appear at string/data addresses and should be treated as tool-emitted local labels unless there is additional evidence for a better manual name.
naming_conventions: SDK/OS symbols use Nintendo-style capitalization and double-underscore runtime names, e.g. `OSDisableInterrupts`, `__OSDBIntegrator`, `__OSSystemCallVectorStart`.
HSD public APIs generally use `HSD_` prefixes, while internal helpers may use unprefixed names such as `MakeTextureMtx`, `TObjSetupMtx`, or `makeMatrix` and be marked local.
Address-derived fallback names remain in subsystem-prefixed forms such as `ftKb_Init_803B7548`, `Ground_803B7DEC`, `grFZeroCar_803B7E50`, `HSD_JObj_803B94C4`, and `AXDriver_803B95F8`.
Compiler-generated locals use forms such as `@230`, `@1395`, and `vec0_init$494`; these names are unstable under regeneration.
dtk-generated local data labels may use ellipsis-style names such as `...data.0`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `dtk dol apply` to reapply current DOL-derived symbol metadata after tooling or analysis updates.
For matching work, rely on address/section/size triples when local generated names churn, since this PR shows many `@NNN` labels changing numbers while staying at the same addresses.

Track function/label aliases at identical addresses, such as `OSDisableInterrupts` and `__RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin` both at `.text:0x80347364`, or `__OSDBINTSTART` and `__OSDBIntegrator` both at `.text:0x8034358C`.
Be cautious when declaring helper functions global: this refresh moved several HSD texture/shadow helpers back to local scope while moving selected conversion helpers to global scope.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR context.
The PR merged very quickly after creation, suggesting it was treated as a straightforward generated-tooling update, but no reviewer rationale is available in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` after running `dtk dol apply`.
Search for decomp PRs affected by `__RAS_OSDisableInterrupts_begin` or other runtime boundary labels.
Look for HSD texture decompilation PRs involving `TObjLoad`, `MakeTextureMtx`, `TObjSetupMtx`, `MakeColorGenTExp`, or `HSD_TexMap2Index` to see how these scope corrections were used.
Search for reviews discussing whether a symbol should be `scope:local` or `scope:global` in `symbols.txt`.
